title: Solution for controlling a target system
abstract: The invention relates to a method for controlling a target system (110), the method comprising: receiving data of at least one source system, training a first machine learning model component (310) with the received data to generate a prediction on a state of the target system (110), generating an uncertainty estimate of the prediction, training a second machine learning model machine learning component (340) with the received data to generate a calibrated uncertainty estimate of the prediction; and the method further comprising: receiving an operational data of the target system (110), controlling the target system (110) by means of selecting a control action by optimization using the first machine learning model component (310) and arranging to apply the calibrated uncertainty estimate generated with the second machine learning model component (340) in the optimization. The invention also relates to a control system and a computer program product.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer-implemented method for controlling a target system (1 10) based on operational data of the target system (1 10), the method comprising: receiving first data of at least one source system, training a first machine learning model component (310) of a machine learning system (240) with the received first data, the first machine learning model component (310) is trained to generate a prediction on a state of the target system (1 10), generating an uncertainty estimate of the prediction, training a second machine learning model component (340) of the machine learning system (240) with second data, the second machine learning model component (340) is trained to generate a calibrated uncertainty estimate of the prediction, the method further comprising: receiving an operational data of the target system (1 10), controlling the target system (1 10) in accordance with the received operational data of the target system (1 10) by means of selecting a control action by optimization using the first machine learning model component (310) and arranging to apply the calibrated uncertainty estimate generated with the second machine learning model component (340) in the optimization. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the uncertainty estimate of the prediction is generated by one of the following: the first machine learning model component (310), the second machine learning model component (340), an external machine learning model component. 3. The computer-implemented method of any of preceding claims, wherein the second machine learning model component (340) of the machine learning system (240) is trained to generate the calibrated uncertainty estimate of the prediction in response to a receipt, as an input to the second machine learning component (340), the following: the prediction on the state of the target system (1 10), the uncertainty estimate of the prediction, and an output of at least one anomaly detector (320). 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 3, wherein the anomaly detector (320) is trained with the first data of at least one source system for detecting deviation in the operational data. 5. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the source system is the same as the target system (110). 6. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the source system is a simulation model corresponding to the target system (1 10). 7. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the source system is a system corresponding to the target system (1 10). 8. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first machine learning model component (310) is one of the following: a neural network, a denoising neural network, a generative adversarial network, a variational autoencoder, a ladder network, a recurrent neural network, a random forest. 9. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the second machine learning model component (340) is one of the 

 following: a neural network, a denoising neural network, a generative adversarial network, a variational autoencoder, a ladder network, a recurrent neural network, a random forest. 10. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the second data is one of the following: the first data; out-of- distribution data. 1 1. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein the out-of- distribution data is generated by one of the following: corrupting the first machine learning model component (310) parameters and generating the out- of-distribution data by evaluating the corrupted first machine learning model component (310); applying abnormal or randomized control signals to the target system (1 10); clustering the first data by process states or operating points. 12. A control system (120) for controlling a target system (1 10) based on operational data of the target system (110), the control system (120) is arranged to: receive first data of at least one source system, train a first machine learning model component (310) of a machine learning system (240) with the received first data, the first machine learning model component (310) is trained to generate a prediction on a state of the target system (1 10), generate an uncertainty estimate of the prediction, train a second machine learning model component (340) of the machine learning system (240) with second data, the second machine learning model component (340) is trained to generate a calibrated uncertainty estimate of the prediction, the control system (120) is further arranged to: 

 receive an operational data of the target system (1 10), control the target system (110) in accordance with the received operational data of the target system (1 10) by means of selecting a control action by optimization using the first machine learning model component (310) and arranging to apply the calibrated uncertainty estimate generated with the second machine learning model component (340) in the optimization. 13. The control system (120) of claim 12, wherein the control system (120) is arranged to generate the uncertainty estimate of the prediction by one of the following: the first machine learning model component (310), the second machine learning model component (340), an external machine learning model component. 14. The control system (120) of preceding claim 12 or 13, wherein the control system (120) is arranged to train the second machine learning model component (340) of the machine learning system (240) to generate the calibrated uncertainty estimate of the prediction in response to a receipt, as an input to the second machine learning component (340), the following: the prediction on the state of the target system (1 10), the uncertainty estimate of the prediction, and an output of at least one anomaly detector (320). 15. The control system (120) of claim 14, wherein the control system (120) is arranged to train the anomaly detector (320) with the first data of at least one source system for detecting deviation in the operational data. 16. The control system (120) of any of preceding claims 12-15, wherein the first machine learning model component (310) is one of the following: a neural network, a denoising neural network, a generative adversarial network, a variational autoencoder, a ladder network, a recurrent neural network, a random forest. 17. The control system (120) of any of preceding claims 12-16, wherein the second machine learning model component (340) is one of the following: a neural network, a denoising neural network, a generative adversarial network, a variational autoencoder, a ladder network, a recurrent neural network, a random forest. 18. The control system (120) of any of the preceding claims 12-17, wherein the second data is one of the following: the first data; out-of-distribution data. 19. The control system (120) of claim 18, wherein the control system (120) is arranged to generate the out-of-distribution data by one of the following: corrupting the first machine learning model component (310) parameters and generating the out-of-distribution data by evaluating the corrupted first machine learning model component (310); applying abnormal or randomized control signals to the target system (1 10); clustering the first data by process states or operating points. 20. A computer program product comprising at least one computer-readable media having computer-executable program code instructions stored therein for performing the method of any of the claims 1 - 11 when the computer program product is executed on a computer.
